vitals.date: 2026: Cancelled (one-year hiatus; planned date was August 2)
vitals.date_specific: 2026 adult edition will not take place — organizer paused after title sponsor withdrew; last held August 3, 2025

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 64. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 61.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — the planned 2026 fifth edition will not take place. When last run (August 3, 2025), the Gran Fondo route measured 132 km / 1,286 m, crossing the Malé Karpaty (Little Carpathians) west of Bratislava and passing through Pezinok and Modra wine country, with the route crossing Pezinská Baba twice and passing near Červený Kameň castle. A separate 70 km / 294 m Medio Fondo shares the same start/finish. Route figures have varied by year — treat these as the last-completed-edition numbers, not a fixed annual course.
course_description.signature_challenge: The double crossing of Pezinská Baba — a real, race-specific pass through the Malé Karpaty, confirmed by both the organizer and the town of Pezinok's own route notice.
suffering_zones: Pezinská Baba (crossed twice) — the defining climb of the route, through the Malé Karpaty above Pezinok
Modra and the Little Carpathians wine country — rolling vineyard roads between the two Pezinská Baba crossings
Červený Kameň area — the route's westernmost extension before turning back toward Bratislava

history.origin_story: Founded in 2022 by organizer CK D3-RACE TEAM Bratislava, running four consecutive adult editions (2022-2025) before a title-sponsor withdrawal forced a one-year pause for the planned fifth (2026) edition. Not part of the UCI Gran Fondo World Series — the organizer describes the event as inspired by the international gran fondo format rather than as a UCI qualifier.

biased_opinion.summary: Granfondo Bratislava ran four consecutive adult editions (2022-2025) as a Slovak regional fondo through the Malé Karpaty wine country, with roughly 500 riders, timing, and a companion Medio Fondo — though per the host town of Pezinok's own municipal notice, the race runs in normal road traffic with a controlled peloton passage rather than full road closures, so riders share the road and must follow ordinary traffic rules once the tail vehicle has passed. On February 17, 2026, organizer CK D3-RACE TEAM announced the planned fifth edition (August 2, 2026) will not take place after title sponsor NAY withdrew support. The organizer frames this explicitly as a pause, not a shutdown, and states an intention to return in 2027 — the separate Junior Fondo continues on schedule for September 2026. This is not a UCI Gran Fondo World Series race and never has been.
strengths: Real, race-specific Malé Karpaty terrain — Pezinská Baba (crossed twice), Modra wine country and the Červený Kameň area, confirmed by both the organizer and local municipal sources
Four consecutive editions (2022-2025) with growing participation (roughly 380 in year one to nearly 500 by 2024-2025) before the pause
Organizer explicitly frames 2026 as a one-year hiatus with a stated intent to return in 2027, not a quiet death
The companion Junior Fondo continues unaffected in September 2026, showing the broader event ecosystem is still active
weaknesses: The planned 2026 adult edition will not take place — this is not currently a bookable race
Return depends on securing new title sponsorship; a stated 2027 intention is not a guarantee
A credible regional Central European fondo (prestige 2/5) with some international participation, not a major destination event or UCI qualifier
Course figures have shifted year to year — the 132 km/1,286 m used here is the last-completed (2025) edition, not a fixed annual number
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A real Slovak fondo with four completed editions through genuine Little Carpathians terrain, run on open roads with controlled peloton passage rather than full closures — just not bookable in 2026. Check back for 2027 sponsorship and revival news before planning around it.

final_verdict.one_liner: Malé Karpaty wine-country fondo, paused for 2026 after its title sponsor pulled out — a real four-edition race, not currently bookable.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Not in 2026 — the organizer has confirmed the planned fifth edition will not take place. If it returns in 2027 as stated, it's worth considering for genuine Little Carpathians terrain and a well-run regional field.
final_verdict.alternatives: For an active Central European wine-country road fondo in the meantime, check the Slovak Cycling Federation's Cycling for All / Merida Road Cup calendar for other regional events.

eligibility.notes: CONFIRMED via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research, editorial wave 8. Organizer's own 17 Feb 2026 statement confirms the planned fifth adult edition (2 Aug 2026) will not take place after title sponsor NAY withdrew — framed explicitly as a one-year pause with a stated intent to return in 2027, not a permanent shutdown. The separate Junior Fondo continues 13 Sep 2026. Corrected this wave: distance_km 99.8->132.0 and elevation_m 1097->1286 (last-completed 2025 edition's official figures); founded 2016->2022 (four editions total: 2022-2025; no evidence of a 2016 origin). Confirmed NOT UCI Gran Fondo World Series affiliated. Pezinská Baba and Malé Karpaty course features confirmed real via organizer and Pezinok municipal sources. SOL REVIEW CORRECTION (2026-07-24): a follow-up sol adversarial review found the initial draft's 'full route closures' claim was unsupported and likely wrong — the City of Pezinok's own municipal notice describes normal road traffic with a controlled peloton passage, with riders required to follow ordinary traffic rules once the tail vehicle has passed. Corrected in biased_opinion accordingly.
